info.title: Financial Datasets API — Analyst Estimates
info.description: Consensus Wall Street analyst estimates (estimated revenue + estimated earnings per share) for US stock tickers, broken down by fiscal period (annual or quarterly). Proxied through AIsa from the Financial Datasets API.

analyst_estimates.description: List of analyst estimate records, one per fiscal period, ordered from most recent.

fiscal_period.description: The fiscal period the estimate applies to, expressed as the period's date. Combine it with `period` to know whether it denotes a fiscal year or a fiscal quarter.

period.description: Whether this record is an annual or a quarterly estimate, matching the requested granularity.

revenue.description:
Consensus estimated total revenue for the fiscal period, in the company's reporting currency.
[⚠️Note:源码未声明金额单位(整额或千/百万),待研发确认。]

earnings_per_share.description: Consensus estimated earnings per share (EPS) for the fiscal period, in the company's reporting currency.

NotFoundError.description: The specified resource was not found

PaymentRequiredError.description: The request requires a paid subscription

example.message: This endpoint requires a paid subscription. Please upgrade your plan.

get.description:
Return consensus Wall Street analyst estimates — projected revenue and earnings per share — for a single US stock ticker, broken down by fiscal period. This is a read-only query: pick annual or quarterly granularity and cap how many periods come back. Typical uses are valuation models, earnings previews, and comparison against actuals.

**Error status codes:** `400`, `401`, `402`, `404`

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Stock ticker symbol of the US-listed company to pull analyst estimates for, e.g. `AAPL`. One ticker per request.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Fiscal granularity of the estimates: `annual` for full-year projections, `quarterly` for per-quarter ones. The list of valid periods can be checked via the `/analyst-estimates/periods` endpoint.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Caps how many of the most recent estimate periods are returned. The accepted ceiling depends on granularity — up to 3 for annual and 12 for quarterly. Higher values are clamped to that ceiling.
